_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning that one letter can make more than one sound, and that every syllable has a vowel sound.

_source.w: We are learning that one letter can make more than one sound, and that every syllable has a vowel sound.
y: Understanding this helps us try different sounds when we get stuck on a word.
Knowing about vowels in syllables helps us break bigger words into manageable parts.
This makes us more flexible and resourceful readers.
eg: We learn that 'c' can sound like 'k' (in 'cat') or 's' (in 'city').
We discover that 'a' makes different sounds in 'cat', 'cake' and 'car'.
We clap the syllables in a word and notice each one has a vowel sound.
We highlight the vowel sound in each syllable of multi-syllable words.
We sort words by whether they use the hard or soft sound of 'g'.
We try both sounds of a letter when a word does not sound right the first time.
